seoTitle: Delete a Deposit in QuickBooks Online Correctly
metaDescription: Learn the exact steps to delete an incorrect deposit in QuickBooks Online so your financial records stay accurate and error-free.
intro: Mistakes with deposits, like a payment entered twice, happen more often than you'd think. This guide shows how to find and delete an incorrect deposit in QuickBooks Online so your accounts stay accurate.

steps.heading: Access your deposits
steps.body: Navigate to the banking or transactions area of QuickBooks Online where deposits are listed.

steps.heading: Select the deposit to delete
steps.body: Locate the specific deposit entry that was made in error and open it to confirm the details before removing it.

steps.heading: Confirm the deletion
steps.body: Use the delete option and confirm the action, which will remove the deposit from your records.

steps.heading: Double-check your records afterward
steps.body: Review your account balance and related transactions to make sure the deletion didn't leave any orphaned entries.

faq.question: Can I undo a deleted deposit?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online keeps an audit trail, so a deleted deposit can typically be identified and manually re-entered if needed, though it isn't restored automatically.

faq.question: Why would I need to delete a deposit?
faq.answer: Common reasons include duplicate entries, incorrect amounts, or deposits recorded to the wrong account.

faq.question: Will deleting a deposit affect linked invoices or payments?
faq.answer: Yes, deleting a deposit can affect related transactions, so it's important to check linked invoices or payments before confirming the deletion.

faq.question: Is this safe for a beginner to do?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as you confirm you're deleting the correct entry, this is a straightforward process suitable for beginners and experienced users alike.
